positions_csv.example_header: Account Number,Account Name,Investment Type,Symbol,Description,Quantity,Last Price,Last Price Change,Current Value,Today's Gain/Loss Dollar,Today's Gain/Loss Percent,Total Gain/Loss Dollar,Total Gain/Loss Percent,Percent Of Account,Cost Basis Total,Average Cost Basis,Type

balances_csv.format_notes: Two-column format: first column is field name, second column is value. Commas as delimiters.

action.notes: BUY, SELL, DIVIDEND RECEIVED, DEBIT CARD PURCHASE, etc.

symbol.notes: May be empty for non-security transactions

action_taxonomy._description: Semantic classification of the Fidelity Action column. Schema (column names + data types) lives above; this block defines MEANING. Applies to transaction_history_csv rows.
action_taxonomy._match_strategy: Uppercase the Action string, then test each category's patterns in declared order. First match wins. Order matters — longer/more-specific patterns precede shorter ones (e.g. 'DIVIDEND RECEIVED SUBSTITUTE PAYMENT' before 'DIVIDEND RECEIVED').

INCOME_WAGES.notes: Payor name is embedded after the keyword (e.g. 'DIRECT DEPOSIT REDACTED_EMPLOYER Inc.PAYROLL'). Extract substring between 'DIRECT DEPOSIT ' and trailing ' (Cash)'/' (Margin)' for employer attribution. Multi-employer households produce several distinct payors per month — preserve attribution, do not aggregate before storage.

INCOME_DIVIDEND.notes: SUBSTITUTE PAYMENT = in-lieu dividend (security was loaned out at record date). Same cash effect as a normal dividend but taxed as ordinary income, not qualified. Check the longer pattern first.

INCOME_CAPITAL_GAIN.notes: Mutual fund / CEF capital-gain distributions. Tag for tax treatment differentiation downstream.

REINVESTMENT.notes: Cash being converted into shares. Income was already booked under DIVIDEND RECEIVED on the matching row — do NOT double-count. Excluded from both income and spending.

MARGIN_INTEREST.notes: Cost of holding margin balance. Track monthly against dividend income to monitor coverage ratio.

INTERNAL_TRANSFER.notes: Movement between Fidelity sub-accounts (cash <-> margin) or between brokers. Net economic effect = $0. MUST be excluded from both income and spending totals.

TRADE_BUY.notes: Securities purchase. Cash outflow but asset stays in portfolio — not spending.

TRADE_SELL.notes: Securities sale. Cash inflow but not income — realized gain/loss is computed elsewhere from cost basis.

DEBIT_CARD_PURCHASE.notes: Merchant name embedded in the Action string after the keyword. Description column is often blank or 'No Description' — parse merchant from Action. Run sub-category matcher (see scripts/data/transaction_processor.py EXPENSE_PATTERNS) for category assignment.

DEBIT_CARD_RETURN.notes: Refund. Offset against the original purchase rather than counted as income.

CASH_WITHDRAWAL.notes: Cash advance from Fidelity margin line. Accrues margin interest (~11%+ APR) — flag for cash-flow analysis.

CC_PAYMENT_PASSTHROUGH.notes: Credit-card payoff. The spending was already captured at the merchant via the CC statement. Counting these as expenses double-counts spending. Only treat as 'spending' if you are NOT also importing CC statements.

AUTO_LOAN_PAYMENT.notes: Wells Fargo 'AUDRAFT' = auto loan payment (not overdraft). Real recurring expense — keep separate from CC pass-throughs.

DIRECT_DEBIT_BILL.notes: Recurring bill payments (loans, tuition, utilities, insurance, HOA). The bare 'DIRECT DEBIT' fallback catches unknown ACH withdrawals — flag those for manual review. Run sub-category matcher against the rest of the Action string.

ELECTRONIC_FUNDS.notes: Generic ACH. Inspect sign and counterparty. Inflows from external bank = potential income; outflows to external bank = potential transfer-out, NOT spending. Default behavior: flag for manual review.

CORPORATE_ACTION.notes: Adjusts share count or cost basis. No income or spending impact.

FEE.notes: Brokerage / transaction fees. Real cost but bucketed separately from lifestyle spending.

income_calculation_rules._description: Aggregation rules for computing 'true income' from classified transactions. Keepfolio dashboards MUST expose wages, investment_income, and total_income as separate metrics — never collapse them into a single number.
income_calculation_rules.investment_income_total: SUM(amount) WHERE category IN (INCOME_DIVIDEND, INCOME_CAPITAL_GAIN, INCOME_INTEREST)
exclusions: REINVESTMENT — cash-to-shares conversion, already counted under DIVIDEND RECEIVED
INTERNAL_TRANSFER (JOURNALED, ACAT) — net zero economic effect
TRADE_SELL — proceeds are not income; realized gain/loss is computed from cost basis elsewhere
DEBIT_CARD_RETURN — refund, offset against the original purchase
CC_PAYMENT_PASSTHROUGH — debt settlement, not new income or spending
income_calculation_rules.employer_attribution: For each INCOME_WAGES row, parse the substring between 'DIRECT DEPOSIT ' and the trailing ' (Cash)'/' (Margin)' marker as the employer label. Group by employer for multi-employer households ({user_name}'s profile has 4+ concurrent payors).

coverage: DIVIDEND RECEIVED SUBSTITUTE PAYMENT (pattern-priority edge case)
DEBIT CARD PURCHASE H-E-B (real spending + sub-category)
regression_fixture.notes: The test contains a reference classifier implementation Keepfolio's TypeScript parser should mirror. Re-run on every parser or taxonomy change to prevent silent double-count regressions.

parsing_notes.currency_format: US format with $ prefix, commas for thousands, periods for decimals. Negative values use parentheses: $(7822.71)
parsing_notes.special_characters: UTF-8 BOM at start of file (\ufeff)

positions: Average Cost Basis must be positive number
balances: Total account value must be positive
Account equity percentage must be between 0-100%
Net debit should be negative if margin is used
